title: Iran Judiciary Threatens to Exclude Protesters from Pardons
detail: Chief Judiciary Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei announced that detained individuals who participated in recent protests would be excluded from the traditional pardons issued for the anniversary of the Islamic Revolution on February 11. The hardline stance came amid ongoing nationwide protests fueled by 60% inflation and a collapsing rial, with thousands arrested since demonstrations erupted in late December 2025.

title: Iran's Top Security Official Returns to Oman
detail: Iran's top security official traveled to Oman for follow-up consultations after the February 6 talks, as both sides engaged in back-channel communications through Omani mediators. The visit underscored Oman's critical role as intermediary and Iran's desire to maintain the diplomatic track while preparing for potential failure. Western officials viewed the continued engagement cautiously, noting Iran's simultaneous acceleration of nuclear fortification.

title: Satellite Images Show Isfahan Nuclear Tunnel Fortification
detail: The Institute for Science and International Security published satellite imagery showing Iran was conducting massive earthworks at the Isfahan nuclear tunnel complex, sealing the middle and southern entrances with huge piles of dirt using heavy engineering equipment. The northern entrance remained open but was reinforced with graduated protective walls designed to block cruise missile penetration. The institute assessed Iran was 'seriously concerned about a US/Israeli aerial attack.'
